message.content:
Research the current landscape of using neural networks for biomedical question answering. Provide a comprehensive but concise overview covering:

1. **Key datasets**: What are the main biomedical QA datasets (e.g., BioASQ, PubMedQA, MedQA, MedMCQA, MMLU medical subsets, etc.)? Include their characteristics (size, type of questions, source).

2. **Main approaches**: 
   - Traditional pre-trained language models (BioBERT, PubMedBERT, BioGPT, etc.)
   - Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approaches
   - Fine-tuning LLMs for biomedical QA
   - Prompting strategies (chain-of-thought, few-shot, etc.)

3. **State-of-the-art models and results**: What are the current best-performing models on major benchmarks?

4. **Key challenges**: Domain-specific terminology, hallucination, evidence grounding, regulatory concerns, etc.

5. **Practical implementation considerations**: What frameworks, libraries, and tools are commonly used?

6. **Recent trends (2024-2025)**: Latest developments in this space.

Please provide this as a structured research summary that can help someone starting a research project in this area.

content.text: I'll conduct a comprehensive research on the current landscape of neural networks for biomedical question answering. Let me search for recent information on this topic.

input.query: biomedical question answering datasets BioASQ PubMedQA MedQA 2024 2025
neural networks biomedical QA state-of-the-art models benchmarks 2025
BioBERT PubMedBERT BioGPT biomedical language models 2024 2025
retrieval augmented generation RAG biomedical question answering 2025
LLM fine-tuning biomedical medical question answering prompting strategies 2025

Web search results for query: "biomedical question answering datasets BioASQ PubMedQA MedQA 2024 2025"

Links: [{"title":"BioASQ datasets","url":"https://participants-area.bioasq.org/datasets/"},{"title":"BioASQ-QA: A manually curated corpus for Biomedical Question Answering - PubMed","url":"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36973320/"},{"title":"GitHub - Andy-jqa/biomedical-qa-datasets: Biomedical Question Answering Datasets.","url":"https://github.com/Andy-jqa/biomedical-qa-datasets"},{"title":"The Challenge | bioasq.org","url":"https://bioasq.org/"},{"title":"BioASQ-QA: A manually curated corpus for Biomedical Question Answering | Scientific Data","url":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41597-023-02068-4"},{"title":"PubMedQA: A Dataset for Biomedical Research Question Answering - ACL Anthology","url":"https://aclanthology.org/D19-1259/"},{"title":"PubMedQA: A Dataset for Biomedical Research Question Answering Qiao Jin","url":"https://users.cs.duke.edu/~bdhingra/papers/pubmedqa.pdf"},{"title":"Biomedical Question Answering: A Survey of Approaches and Challenges | ACM Computing Surveys","url":"https://dl.acm.org/doi/10.1145/3490238"},{"title":"[1909.06146] PubMedQA: A Dataset for Biomedical Research Question Answering","url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.06146"},{"title":"PubMedQA Homepage","url":"https://pubmedqa.github.io/"}]

Here are the search results for biomedical question answering datasets:

## BioASQ

The BioASQ question answering (QA) benchmark dataset contains questions in English, along with golden standard (reference) answers and related material, designed to reflect real information needs of biomedical experts and is therefore more realistic and challenging than most existing datasets. The BioASQ-QA dataset also includes ideal answers (in effect summaries), which are particularly useful for research on multi-document summarization. There are 4 types of QA instances in BioASQ: factoid, list, yes/no and summary, with exact answers being short answers that directly answer the questions (single and multiple biomedical entities for factoid and list questions, respectively; "yes" or "no" for yes/no questions).

The training data for the Synergy 2024 task and the Synergy 2025 task are available.

## PubMedQA

PubMedQA is a novel biomedical question answering (QA) dataset collected from PubMed abstracts. The task of PubMedQA is to answer research questions with yes/no/maybe (e.g.: Do preoperative statins reduce atrial fibrillation after coronary artery bypass grafting?) PubMedQA has 1k expert-annotated, 61.2k unlabeled and 211.3k artificially generated QA instances.

## MedQA

MEDQA is the first free-form multiple-choice OpenQA dataset for solving medical problems, which is collected from the professional medical board exams, and covers three languages: English, simplified Chinese, and traditional Chinese, containing 12,723, 34,251, and 14,123 questions for the three languages, respectively.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "BioBERT PubMedBERT BioGPT biomedical language models 2024 2025"

Links: [{"title":"BioBERT: a pre-trained biomedical language representation model for biomedical text mining - PMC","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC7703786/"},{"title":"BioBERT: a pre-trained biomedical language representation model for biomedical text mining - PubMed","url":"https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31501885/"},{"title":"GitHub - dmis-lab/biobert: Bioinformatics'2020: BioBERT: a pre-trained biomedical language representation model for biomedical text mining","url":"https://github.com/dmis-lab/biobert"},{"title":"GitHub - naver/biobert-pretrained: BioBERT: a pre-trained biomedical language representation model for biomedical text mining","url":"https://github.com/naver/biobert-pretrained"},{"title":"BioBERT vs. PubMedBERT. Model Comparison | by Eleventh Hour Enthusiast | Medium","url":"https://medium.com/@EleventhHourEnthusiast/model-comparison-biobert-vs-pubmedbert-8c2d78178d10"},{"title":"Benchmarking large language models for biomedical natural language processing applications and recommendations | Nature Communications","url":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-025-56989-2"},{"title":"[1901.08746] BioBERT: a pre-trained biomedical language representation model for biomedical text mining","url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/1901.08746"},{"title":"Large Language Models in Bio-Ontology Research: A Review - PMC","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12649945/"},{"title":"BioBERT: a pre-trained biomedical language representation model for biomedical text mining | Bioinformatics | Oxford Academic","url":"https://academic.oup.com/bioinformatics/article/36/4/1234/5566506"},{"title":"Large language models to process, analyze, and synthesize biomedical texts: a scoping review | Discover Artificial Intelligence | Springer Nature Link","url":"https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s44163-024-00197-2"}]

I've completed the web search for biomedical language models. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## BioBERT

BioBERT is a pre-trained language representation model for the biomedical domain. It is initialized with weights from BERT pre-trained on general domain corpora (English Wikipedia and BooksCorpus), then BioBERT is pre-trained on biomedical domain corpora (PubMed abstracts and PMC full-text articles). BioBERT significantly outperforms BERT on biomedical named entity recognition (0.62% F1 score improvement), biomedical relation extraction (2.80% F1 score improvement) and biomedical question answering (12.24% MRR improvement).

## PubMedBERT

Unlike BioBERT, PubMedBERT is trained from scratch solely on biomedical text, allowing it to develop a highly specialized understanding of the domain. PubMedBERT employs a custom vocabulary that is generated specifically from biomedical text, which allows PubMedBERT to better represent and understand biomedical terms compared to models that use a general-purpose vocabulary.

## BioGPT

BioGPT is a generative transformer model which, after being pre-trained on an extensive biomedical literature corpus, demonstrates versatility across six different BioNLP challenges. BioGPT is a decoder-based, generative language model using the decoder from the transformer architecture and belongs to the generative pre-trained transformer (GPT) family.

## Recent Benchmarking (2024-2025)

For extractive and classification tasks, state-of-the-art approaches fine-tuned biomedical domain-specific BERT models such as BioBERT and PubMedBERT. Domain-adapted biomedical language models such as BioBERT, SciBERT, PubMedBERT, and BioGPT provide stronger in-domain grounding that is directly relevant to ontology engineering tasks.

Web search results for query: "neural networks biomedical QA state-of-the-art models benchmarks 2025"

Links: [{"title":"Benchmarking large language models for biomedical natural language processing applications and recommendations | Nature Communications","url":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-025-56989-2"},{"title":"Research Hui Liu a,⁎ Engineering 50 (2025) 270–282","url":"https://www.engineering.org.cn/engi/EN/PDF/10.1016/j.eng.2025.02.010"},{"title":"Journal of Information Systems Engineering and Management 2025, 10(26s)","url":"https://jisem-journal.com/index.php/journal/article/download/4241/1878/6971"},{"title":"Questioning Our Questions: How Well Do Medical QA Benchmarks Evaluate Clinical Capabilities of Language Models? - ACL Anthology","url":"https://aclanthology.org/2025.bionlp-1.24/"},{"title":"MedBioRAG: Semantic Search and Retrieval-Augmented Generation with","url":"https://www.arxiv.org/pdf/2512.10996"},{"title":"Generative AI Models in Time-Varying Biomedical Data: Scoping Review - PMC","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11933772/"},{"title":"Review of deep learning models with Spiking Neural ...","url":"https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/neuroscience/articles/10.3389/fnins.2025.1623497/pdf"},{"title":"Benchmarking for biomedical natural language processing tasks with a domain specific ALBERT | BMC Bioinformatics | Full Text","url":"https://bmcbioinformatics.biomedcentral.com/articles/10.1186/s12859-022-04688-w"},{"title":"Benchmarking deep learning models on large healthcare datasets - ScienceDirect","url":"https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1532046418300716"},{"title":"Building an LLM Agent for Life Sciences Literature QA and ...","url":"https://journalwjarr.com/sites/default/files/fulltext_pdf/WJARR-2025-1665.pdf"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about neural networks for biomedical question answering, state-of-the-art models, and benchmarks in 2025:

## Key Findings

### State-of-the-Art Models and Performance

GPT-4 and Med-PaLM 2 have demonstrated high performance on MedQA, with accuracy scores of 86.1 and 86.5, respectively, compared with an average of 87.0 for human experts.

Closed-source LLMs like GPT-4 excel in reasoning-related tasks such as medical question answering. However, traditional fine-tuning outperforms zero- or few-shot LLMs in most tasks.

A recent model called MedBioRAG consistently outperforms previous SoTA models and GPT-4o across multiple biomedical QA benchmarks.

### Major Benchmarks and Datasets

Key benchmarks for evaluating biomedical QA systems include:
- MedQA (based on the US medical licensing exam) and PubMedQA (based on PubMed citation abstracts)
- BioASQ, PubMedQA, and MedQA datasets
- NFCorpus and TREC-COVID

### Current Challenges

Recent advances in large language models have led to impressive performance on medical question-answering benchmarks, however, the extent to which these benchmarks reflect real-world clinical capabilities remains uncertain.

### Neural Architecture Trends

The development of neural transformer architectures has further transformed the medical QA field. Additionally, GREASELM combines pre-trained language models with Graph Neural Networks to enhance context-aware reasoning in biomedical question answering.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: "retrieval augmented generation RAG biomedical question answering 2025"

Links: [{"title":"[2505.07917] Efficient and Reproducible Biomedical Question Answering using Retrieval Augmented Generation","url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.07917"},{"title":"RAG-BioQA: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Long-Form Biomedical Question Answering","url":"https://arxiv.org/html/2510.01612v1"},{"title":"Efficient and Reproducible Biomedical Question Answering using Retrieval Augmented Generation","url":"https://arxiv.org/html/2505.07917"},{"title":"Rationale-Guided Retrieval Augmented Generation for Medical Question Answering - ACL Anthology","url":"https://aclanthology.org/2025.naacl-long.635/"},{"title":"[2509.05505] Biomedical Literature Q&A System Using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)","url":"https://arxiv.org/abs/2509.05505"},{"title":"A survey on retrieval-augmentation generation (RAG) models for healthcare applications | Neural Computing and Applications | Springer Nature Link","url":"https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00521-025-11666-9"},{"title":"Retrieval-Augmented Generation: A Comprehensive Survey of Architectures, Enhancements, and Robustness Frontiers","url":"https://arxiv.org/html/2506.00054v1"},{"title":"Rationale-Guided Retrieval Augmented Generation for ...","url":"https://aclanthology.org/2025.naacl-long.635.pdf"},{"title":"An Optimized Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Framework for Biomedical Question Answering: Enhancing Semantic Alignment and Factual Accuracy via HyDE, BioBERT Re-ranking, and Targeted Prompt Engineering by Qishan Chen, Zichun Su, MINGYU TAN, Wenxi Xu :: SSRN","url":"https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=5934360"},{"title":"(PDF) RAG-BioQA Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Long-Form Biomedical Question Answering","url":"https://www.researchgate.net/publication/396143141_RAG-BioQA_Retrieval-Augmented_Generation_for_Long-Form_Biomedical_Question_Answering"}]

Here's a comprehensive overview of the search results on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for biomedical question answering in 2025:

## Key Recent Developments

RAG models have become crucial in healthcare applications, significantly enhancing the relevance and reliability of AI-driven insights by combining the generative capabilities of large language models (LLMs) with retrieval-based methods. RAG models address critical limitations of LLMs, such as the tendency to "hallucinate" or produce inaccurate information by incorporating real-time retrieval from trusted medical knowledge bases and clinical literature, ensuring that responses are both contextually accurate and aligned with the latest clinical evidence.

## Notable 2025 Research Systems

Several cutting-edge RAG systems for biomedical QA have emerged:

**Rationale-Guided RAG (RAG²)**: This system incorporates a small filtering model trained on perplexity-based labels of rationales, LLM-generated rationales as queries, and a structure designed to retrieve snippets evenly from a comprehensive set of four biomedical corpora, achieving improvements of up to 6.1% and outperforming the previous best medical RAG model by up to 5.6%.

**Efficient Biomedical RAG**: Evaluations show that retrieving 50 documents with BM25 before reranking with MedCPT optimally balances accuracy (0.90), recall (0.90), and response time (1.91s).

**Long-Form Biomedical QA (RAG-BioQA)**: The framework consists of three main components: (1) a preprocessing pipeline for dataset preparation and embedding generation, (2) a retrieval module with re-ranking strategies, and (3) an answer generation module based on fine-tuned T5 models.

## Benchmarking and Evaluation

MIRAGE, a benchmark tailored to medical RAG, contains 7,663 questions sourced from five clinical and biomedical QA datasets and incorporates real-world evaluation constraints: zero-shot generalization, multiple-choice formats, retrieval necessity assessment, and question-only retrieval.

These 2025 advances demonstrate significant progress in making RAG systems more efficient, accurate, and scalable for biomedical applications.

Web search results for query: "LLM fine-tuning biomedical medical question answering prompting strategies 2025"

Links: [{"title":"OpenMedLM: prompt engineering can out-perform fine-tuning in medical question-answering with open-source large language models | Scientific Reports","url":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-024-64827-6"},{"title":"Journal of Medical Internet Research - Large Language Model Synergy for Ensemble Learning in Medical Question Answering: Design and Evaluation Study","url":"https://www.jmir.org/2025/1/e70080"},{"title":"The Economics of Accuracy for Medical Reasoning with Large Language Models","url":"https://www.medrxiv.org/content/10.64898/2025.12.22.25342804v1.full.pdf"},{"title":"Original Paper Large Language Model Synergy for Ensemble Learning in","url":"https://www.jmir.org/2025/1/e70080/PDF"},{"title":"Large language models encode clinical knowledge | Nature","url":"https://www.nature.com/articles/s41586-023-06291-2"},{"title":"OpenMedLM: prompt engineering can out-perform fine-tuning in medical question-answering with open-source large language models - PMC","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11187169/"},{"title":"Fine-tuning medical language models for enhanced long-contextual understanding and domain expertise - PMC","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12209640/"},{"title":"Do Clinical Question Answering Systems Really Need Specialised Medical Fine Tuning?","url":"https://arxiv.org/html/2601.12812"},{"title":"Original Paper Fine-Tuning Methods for Large Language Models in Clinical","url":"https://www.jmir.org/2025/1/e76048/PDF"},{"title":"Large Language Models in Healthcare and Medical Applications: A Review - PMC","url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC12189880/"}]

I've performed the web search for your query on LLM fine-tuning, biomedical/medical question answering, and prompting strategies for 2025. Here are the key findings:

## Recent 2025 Research Highlights

**Ensemble Learning & Prompting Approaches:**
The LLM-Synergy framework using ensemble methods represents a significant advancement in leveraging LLMs for medical QA tasks, providing a flexible and efficient strategy adaptable to current and future challenges in biomedical informatics.

**Prompting vs. Fine-Tuning:**
OpenMedLM delivered state-of-the-art results on three medical LLM benchmarks, surpassing previous best-performing open-source models that leveraged costly and extensive fine-tuning. Prompting strategies included zero-shot, few-shot, chain-of-thought, and ensemble/self-consistency voting.

**Fine-Tuning Methods:**
With supervised fine-tuning (SFT), model accuracies increased to 28-33%, and with direct preference optimization (DPO), accuracies increased further to 36-40% for the MedQA dataset.

**Fine-Tuning Approaches in Medical Domain:**
BenTsao introduced a method that integrates structured medical knowledge bases to fine-tune existing LLMs, enabling the model to refer to authoritative medical information when generating responses. IvyGPT proposed mixing real question answering data with generated data for fine-tuning to expand the effective training amount.

The search results show an active research landscape in 2025 with focus on both traditional fine-tuning and emerging prompting strategies for biomedical QA tasks.
